title: Date squares
subtitle: Oat traybake with sticky date filling
excerpt: A traybake with a buttery oat base and crumble top sandwiching a thick, sweet date paste. Simple to make, keeps well for a week, and cuts into neat squares.
sourceNotes: Date squares (also known as matrimonial cake in Canada) appear in British and Canadian domestic cookery from the early twentieth century. The formula (an oat and butter crumble layer sandwiching a cooked date filling) is consistent across regional variants. This version follows the standard British domestic formula.

recipe.freezeNotes: Wrap individual squares. Freeze for up to 3 months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps well for a week in a tin. The date filling continues to soften into the oat layers.

glossaryTerms.definition: Dates cooked with a little water until they break down into a thick, spreadable paste. The natural sugars caramelise slightly as they cook. Medjool dates give the most flavour, but standard dried pitted dates work perfectly well and are much cheaper.

content.text: Date squares are built in three steps: cook the date filling, make the oat mixture, assemble. The filling and the oat base both have the same ten-minute prep; the oven does the rest. Cut only when fully cooled — the filling is liquid while warm and the squares crumble if you rush it.
Put the {{dried-dates}} in a small saucepan with 150 ml of water and the {{lemon-juice}}. Cook over medium heat, stirring, for 8–10 minutes until the dates break down into a thick
. Remove from the heat and leave to cool.
Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Grease a 20×20 cm tin and line with baking paper.

Mix the {{porridge-oats}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{fine-sea-salt}}, and {{bicarbonate-of-soda}} together in a large bowl. Add the cold {{unsalted-butter}} and rub in with your fingertips until the mixture comes together in rough, crumbly clumps.

Press half the oat mixture into the base of the tin in a firm, even layer. Spread the date paste over it, reaching the edges. Scatter the remaining oat mixture on top and press it down gently. Bake for 25–30 minutes until the top is golden. Cool completely in the tin before cutting into squares.
